← All good news The Planet

For the First Time Ever, the World Made More Electricity From Wind and Sun Than Gas

In April 2026, wind and solar produced 531 terawatt-hours of power worldwide — overtaking gas for the first time.

via Ember · 6 hours ago

⚡

Energy think tank Ember crunched the numbers for April 2026 and found a genuine milestone: wind and solar generated 531 terawatt-hours of electricity that month, about 54 more than gas — the first time the two renewables out-produced gas across the whole world.

It's a single month, but it marks a line crossed. After more than two decades of record-setting growth, clean power isn't the future of the grid anymore so much as its present.

Read the full story at Ember →

How Cloudbreak works

We comb publishers across the web for the stories that make your day a little brighter, show you the headline and a quick summary, then send you straight to the original source for the full story. We don't republish anyone's work — good news belongs to everyone, and credit belongs to the people who reported it.

Spotted something good?

A neighbor, a rescue, a small win on your street — if it made you smile, send it our way.
